evidence: src/__init__.py:1-7 — entire file is three metadata constants; no __all__, no package-level imports, no version guard
diagnosis: Package init provides only cosmetic metadata; it is not actively harmful but gives no protection against accidental wildcard imports or version mismatch.
proposed_fix: Add __all__ = [] to prevent wildcard import leakage, and a runtime Python version guard: import sys; assert sys.version_info >= (3, 10), 'Python 3.10+ required'. No other changes needed.
test_plan: Import the package in a Python 3.9 environment and assert ImportError is raised; import in 3.10+ and assert no error.

evidence: src/k8s_manager.py:22-23 — 'self.client = None; self.api_client = None'; line 29 — '# Kubernetes client initialization would go here / # For now, we\'ll use a stub implementation'; lines 49-88 — get_cluster_info returns hardcoded dict with 'v1.29.0', nodes:3, pods:45 regardless of any real cluster

Rewrite get_cluster_info to call self.client.list_node(), list_namespace(), list_pod_for_all_namespaces(). evidence: src/enhanced_tools.py:130-160 — kubectl_logs Tool schema defined; grep of src/mcp_server.py for 'kubectl_logs' returns zero hits — never imported or registered
diagnosis: kubectl_logs schema exists in enhanced_tools.py but is not wired into mcp_server.py, so MCP clients cannot call it even though the backing method kubectl_manager.kubectl_logs exists at kubectl_manager.py:170.

evidence: Entire src/ directory has no Finding dataclass or serialization module (grep for 'Finding', 'DPO', 'pydantic', 'dataclass' returns zero hits in src/); remediation results are untyped dicts

Add to_dict() / from_dict() and to_jsonl_line() methods. All managers should return List[Finding] instead of untyped dicts. Add a DPOPair(prompt: str, chosen: str, rejected: str) dataclass alongside.

evidence: VSCODE_K8S_INTEGRATION.md:55 — 'curl http://172.100.10.107:30001/health' (hardcoded private IP); line 244 — '{"status":"healthy",...}' (claimed health response); line 102 — '@kubernetes-platform-engineer diagnose my cluster health' (implied real cluster data); src/k8s_manager.py:49-88 — all cluster data is hardcoded stubs

Commands shown will succeed technically but cluster values are not live.' Remove or annotate the hardcoded IP 172.100.10.107 as a placeholder. Update 'Success Indicators' item 4 to reflect stub status.

evidence: logs.sh:1-3 — no 'set -euo pipefail'; no shebang validation; hardcoded container name 'k8s-mcp-server' with no fallback if container is absent; no error handling if docker is not installed
diagnosis: logs.sh is a one-liner with no error handling, no pipefail guard, and a hardcoded container name that will silently fail with a confusing docker error if the container does not exist.
proposed_fix:
Replace file content with: #!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
CONTAINER=${MCP_CONTAINER:-k8s-mcp-server}
if ! docker ps -q --filter "name=${CONTAINER}" | grep -q .; then
  echo "ERROR: container ${CONTAINER} not running" >&2; exit 1
fi
docker logs "${CONTAINER}" -f
test_plan: Run with a non-existent container name and verify exit code 1 with a clear error message; run with container name set via env and verify logs stream correctly.

evidence: Entire src/ directory has no state machine, no review/PRD/split/implement/verify loop, no stop-condition logic (grep for 'state_machine', 'iteration', 'PRD', 'stop_condition' returns zero hits)
diagnosis: The review->PRD->split->implement->verify iteration loop does not exist anywhere in the codebase; the auto-remediator has no way to manage multi-step repair cycles or stop safely.
proposed_fix: Create src/iteration_engine.py with IterationStateMachine. States: IDLE, REVIEWING, PRD_GENERATION, TASK_SPLIT, IMPLEMENTING, VERIFYING, STOPPED. Transitions: REVIEWING->PRD_GENERATION (findings >0), PRD_GENERATION->TASK_SPLIT (PRD approved), TASK_SPLIT->IMPLEMENTING (tasks assigned), IMPLEMENTING->VERIFYING (all tasks done), VERIFYING->IDLE (all tests pass) or VERIFYING->IMPLEMENTING (regressions found, max_retries not exceeded). Stop conditions: max_iterations exceeded, no findings remain, operator STOP signal. Persist state to state.json for crash recovery.
test_plan: Unit-test each transition with mock finding lists; assert IMPLEMENTING->VERIFYING only fires when all tasks in DONE state; assert STOPPED is reached when max_iterations=2 and iteration counter hits 2.

evidence: status.sh:1 — '#!/bin/bash' with no 'set -euo pipefail'; status.sh:3 — 'if docker ps | grep -q k8s-mcp-server'; status.sh:5 — 'echo "✓ Health: OK (Docker healthcheck passed)"' (hardcoded assertion, not probed)
diagnosis: status.sh lacks set -euo pipefail so any command failure is silently swallowed, and it prints a hardcoded health assertion rather than querying the actual Docker health status.
proposed_fix: Add 'set -euo pipefail' after shebang. Replace the hardcoded health echo with: health=$(docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}' k8s-mcp-server 2>/dev/null || echo 'unavailable'); echo "Health: $health". Remove the literal '✓ Health: OK' line.
test_plan: Run status.sh against a stopped container and verify non-zero exit code and no false 'OK' output. Run against a healthy container and verify actual Docker health status is shown.

evidence: No dedup logic exists anywhere in src/; diagnostics_manager.py returns flat lists of issues with no grouping by root cause; monitoring_manager.py returns parallel lists of bottlenecks with no identity keys.
diagnosis: There is no mechanism to group or deduplicate N findings that share the same root cause across multiple resources, so callers receive noisy repeated findings.
proposed_fix: Data model: FindingKey(kind: str, message_template: str, affected_resources: list[str]). Interface: def dedup_findings(findings: list[Finding]) -> list[Finding] groups by (kind, normalized_message) and merges affected_resources lists, keeping highest severity. Dependencies: hashlib for fingerprint, difflib.SequenceMatcher for fuzzy message similarity (threshold 0.85). Edge cases: same message different namespaces should remain separate; findings with no message should never merge. Implementation location: new src/finding_dedup.py, called by diagnostics_manager before returning results.
test_plan: Unit test: inject 10 findings with identical (kind, message) across 10 pod names; assert output is 1 finding with 10 affected_resources. Second test: inject findings with dissimilar messages; assert no merging occurs.

evidence: src/enhanced_tools.py:15 — Tool(name='kubectl_get', ...); grep of entire repo for 'import enhanced_tools' or 'from enhanced_tools' returns zero matches outside enhanced_tools.py itself; src/mcp_server.py has no import of enhanced_tools (confirmed by grep returning no output)

In handle_list_tools(), extend the returned tools list with get_enhanced_kubectl_tools(). In handle_call_tool(), add: 'elif name == "kubectl_get": result = await self.kubectl_manager.get_resources(resource=arguments["resource"], name=arguments.get("name"), namespace=arguments.get("namespace"), output_format=arguments.get("output_format","yaml"))'. Implement kubectl_manager.get_resources() using kubernetes Python client list APIs.
test_plan: Call the 'kubectl_get' tool via the MCP test harness; confirm the handler is reached and a real API call (or mock) is dispatched. Confirm kubectl_get appears in the list_tools response.

evidence: src/mcp_server.py:508-513 — routes to self.monitoring_manager.analyze_resource_usage(); src/monitoring_manager.py:59 — comment '# Stub implementation for different scopes'; monitoring_manager.py:199-203 — 'total_cores: 12, used_cores: 5.4' are literal constants; monitoring_manager.py:55 — 'timestamp: 2025-08-09T10:30:00Z' is a hardcoded past date; no kubernetes or metrics_server client is imported or called anywhere in monitoring_manager.py

proposed_fix: Import kubernetes.client.CustomObjectsApi in monitoring_manager.py. For cluster/node scope call custom_objects_api.list_cluster_custom_object('metrics.k8s.io','v1beta1','nodes'). For namespace/pod scope call custom_objects_api.list_namespaced_custom_object('metrics.k8s.io','v1beta1',namespace,'pods'). Parse the response to build the analysis dict dynamically. evidence: monitoring_manager.py:107-113 — log_summary hardcodes 'error_count: 23, warning_count: 67'; monitoring_manager.py:115-128 — top_errors list is static with dates '2025-08-09'; monitoring_manager.py:258-272 — _analyze_cluster_performance returns literal 'avg_response_time: 45ms'; monitoring_manager.py — zero imports of kubernetes, subprocess, or any observability client
diagnosis: MonitoringManager implements no real logic in any method — all three public methods (analyze_resource_usage, analyze_logs, performance_analysis) and all private helpers return hardcoded fake data with no k8s API calls.
proposed_fix: For analyze_logs: use kubernetes.client.CoreV1Api.read_namespaced_pod_log(pod, namespace, since_seconds=parse_time_range(time_range)) to fetch real logs; apply log_level and search_pattern filters in Python. For performance_analysis: call metrics-server custom objects API. Replace all nine stub helper methods with real API calls. Remove hardcoded literal dicts entirely.

evidence: src/enhanced_tools.py:604 — Tool(name='helm_status', ...) defined in get_enhanced_helm_tools(); grep of repo for 'import enhanced_tools' or 'from enhanced_tools' returns zero matches outside enhanced_tools.py; src/mcp_server.py — no import of enhanced_tools, no 'helm_status' in handle_call_tool dispatch

evidence: diagnostics_manager.py:55 — comment '# Stub implementation for different check types'; diagnostics_manager.py:110 — 'results.update({"status": "CrashLoopBackOff", "restart_count": 5, ...})' applied unconditionally regardless of actual pod_name; diagnostics_manager.py:217-275 — _check_nodes/_check_pods/_check_services/_check_networking/_check_storage all return hardcoded dict literals with no kubernetes client calls

(hardcoded path with mangled username, missing dot before com); setup-vscode-k8s.sh:8 — 'VSCODE_SETTINGS_DIR="$HOME/Library/Application Support/Code/User"' correctly uses $HOME but the inline Python block on line 77/89 ignores it and uses a different broken literal path; setup-vscode-k8s.sh:33 — 'read -p' makes script non-interactive in CI

Also add a '--non-interactive' flag that skips the read -p prompt and defaults to abort, for CI usage.

evidence: No HPAAnalyzer exists in src/; no HPA-related analysis appears in diagnostics_manager.py, monitoring_manager.py, or k8s_manager.py; Kubernetes HPA object has spec.minReplicas, spec.maxReplicas, status.currentMetrics, status.conditions fields that cover all three scenarios.
diagnosis: No HPA analysis capability exists; the PRD must specify how to detect and surface the three failure modes: missing metrics source, replicas capped at max, and replicas stuck at zero despite load.
proposed_fix: Data model: HPAFinding(name, namespace, condition: Literal['no_metrics','capped_at_max','never_scaling'], current_replicas, max_replicas, metric_source, last_scale_time). Interface: async def analyze_hpa(namespace=None) -> list[HPAFinding]. Implementation: call autoscaling_v2_api.list_namespaced_horizontal_pod_autoscaler(namespace). For each HPA: (a) if status.conditions contains type=ScalingActive reason=FailedGetScale -> no_metrics; (b) if currentReplicas==maxReplicas for >threshold_minutes -> capped_at_max; (c) if lastScaleTime is None or older than staleness_threshold and currentReplicas==minReplicas -> never_scaling. Edge cases: HPA with external metrics, HPA v1 vs v2 schema differences, missing lastScaleTime on new HPAs.
test_plan: Unit test with mocked autoscaling API responses covering each of the three conditions. Integration test with a kind cluster HPA targeting a deployment with no metrics-server; assert no_metrics finding is returned.

evidence: github_issues_manager.py:56 — 'self.github_token = None  # Add GitHub token for higher rate limits' (token is hardcoded to None, never read from env or config); github_issues_manager.py:488 — 'search_terms = " OR ".join([f"title LIKE \'%{term}%\' OR body LIKE \'%{term}%\'" for term in key_terms])' with f-string interpolation directly into SQL (SQL injection via key_terms); github_issues_manager.py:320 — 'issue["reactions"]["total_count"]' will raise KeyError if GitHub returns issue without reactions field
diagnosis: GitHubIssuesManager has three distinct bugs: GitHub token is hardwired to None (always unauthenticated), find_similar_issues constructs SQL by string interpolation creating a SQL injection vector, and _extract_issue_data will crash on any issue missing the 'reactions' key.
proposed_fix: Token: replace line 56 with 'self.github_token = os.getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN") or getattr(config, "github_token", None)'. SQL injection: replace the f-string SQL build in find_similar_issues with parameterized queries using '?' placeholders and pass key_terms as params list. KeyError: replace line 320 with 'issue.get("reactions", {}).get("total_count", 0)'.
test_plan: Set GITHUB_TOKEN env var; verify Authorization header is sent in HTTP requests. Call find_similar_issues with a key_term containing a single quote (e.g. "O'Reilly"); verify no SQL error. Pass a mock issue dict without 'reactions' key to _extract_issue_data; verify it completes without exception.

evidence: src/monitoring_manager.py:99-141 — 'total_entries': 1247, 'filtered_entries': 156, ... 'error_count': 23, ... 'message': 'Failed to connect to database'
diagnosis: analyze_logs is wired in mcp_server.py line 523-530 and calls monitoring_manager.analyze_logs, but that method returns entirely hardcoded fake log counts, error messages, and timestamps — no kubernetes client call or log aggregation is performed.
proposed_fix: Replace stub body with real implementation: (1) use kubernetes.client.CoreV1Api().read_namespaced_pod_log() to fetch logs for pod targets; (2) parse log lines by level and match search_pattern via re.search; (3) compute real counts from actual log stream; (4) remove hardcoded 'total_entries', 'error_count', and canned error message strings.
test_plan: Deploy a test pod that emits known error/warn/info lines; call analyze_logs targeting that pod and assert returned counts match actual log line counts; assert hardcoded string 'Failed to connect to database' is absent from output when logs do not contain it.

evidence: tests/production/test_pod_issues.py:70 — 'mcp_server.resolve_issue_end_to_end = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_pipeline_result)'; line 150 — 'github_issues_manager.find_similar_issues = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_similar_issues)'; every test replaces the method under test with AsyncMock before calling it.
diagnosis: Every test in this file mocks the exact function being tested and then asserts on the mock return value, meaning no real code path is exercised — all 10 tests are self-validating stubs that cannot catch regressions.
proposed_fix: Remove AsyncMock replacement of production methods; instead mock only external dependencies (kubernetes client, GitHub API). Wire tests to real manager instances via dependency injection. Use a fake k8s client (e.g., kubernetes-fake) to supply controlled pod/event data so assertions reflect actual code logic.

evidence: N/A — design task; current execute_remediation stub (k8s_manager.py:166-210) has no probe_tuning case.
diagnosis: No implementation exists for deterministic probe tuning remediation; a real auto-remediator needs a concrete data model, interface, and edge case handling to fix connection-refused startup failures.
proposed_fix: Data model: ProbeTuningSpec(pod_name, namespace, container_name, current_initial_delay_seconds, recommended_initial_delay_seconds, probe_type: Literal['liveness','readiness','startup']). Interface: async def tune_probe(spec: ProbeTuningSpec, dry_run: bool) -> ProbeTuningResult. Implementation: (1) fetch pod spec via read_namespaced_pod; (2) detect connection-refused pattern in recent events (reason=Unhealthy, message contains 'connection refused'); (3) compute new initialDelaySeconds = max(current+10, observed_startup_time+5); (4) patch pod's owning Deployment via strategic merge patch. Edge cases: pod not owned by Deployment (abort), initial_delay already > 120s (flag for manual review), multiple containers (require explicit container_name).
test_plan: Unit test with fake k8s client: create deployment with initialDelaySeconds=5, inject Unhealthy events, call tune_probe, assert patch is issued with initialDelaySeconds>=15; assert dry_run=True produces no patch call; assert unowned pod raises ValueError.

evidence: deploy-k8s.sh:6 — 'set -e' (missing -u and -o pipefail); line 63 — 'CLUSTER_SERVER=$(kubectl cluster-info | grep "control plane" | awk ...)' (pipe drops exit codes); line 146 — 'kubectl port-forward ... &' backgrounded process whose PID is killed at line 158 without checking if tests passed; no idempotency guard before 'docker build'.
diagnosis: deploy-k8s.sh uses only 'set -e', leaving unbound variable references and broken pipes silently ignored; piped commands on line 63 can silently produce empty variables; the background port-forward and unconditional kill pattern can mask real test failures.
proposed_fix: Change line 6 to 'set -euo pipefail'. On line 63 replace the awk pipe chain with: 'CLUSTER_SERVER=$(kubectl cluster-info --short 2>/dev/null | grep "control plane" | awk "{print \$NF}")'. Wrap the port-forward test block in a function that propagates the curl exit code before killing the port-forward PID. Add idempotency for docker build: 'docker build -t k8s-platform-engineer-mcp:latest .' only when explicitly requested.
test_plan: Run shellcheck against the updated script and confirm zero SC2086/SC2039/SC2069 errors; run with an intentionally missing kubeconfig and verify the script exits non-zero with an explicit error message rather than continuing silently.

evidence: setup-vscode-k8s.sh:77 — hardcoded path '/Users/hawaiidevelopergmail.com/Library/Application Support/Code/User/settings.json' (missing @ in email); line 89 — same hardcoded path used for write; line 7 — 'VSCODE_SETTINGS_DIR' set via $HOME but Python block ignores it and uses a literal path.
diagnosis: The embedded Python block hardcodes a malformed user path ('/Users/hawaiidevelopergmail.com') that is almost certainly wrong on any real machine, meaning the VS Code settings merge silently fails or corrupts another user's settings on any machine except the original developer's.
proposed_fix: Remove the hardcoded path from the heredoc Python block. Pass the settings file path as an environment variable: 'SETTINGS_FILE_PATH="$SETTINGS_FILE" python3 - <<EOF' and inside the Python block use 'import os; settings_file = os.environ["SETTINGS_FILE_PATH"]'. This makes the script work for any user whose $HOME is correctly set by the shell.

evidence: setup-vscode-nodeport.sh:13-14 — 'CLUSTER_IP=$(kubectl get nodes ...)' and 'NODEPORT=$(kubectl get svc ...)' run before any cluster connectivity check; line 30 — 'exit 1' if health check fails, but the NodePort variables could already be empty strings if kubectl commands failed silently; line 6 — 'set -euo pipefail' is present (good), but empty CLUSTER_IP/NODEPORT on kubectl failure means errexit may not fire if kubectl exits 0 with empty output.
diagnosis: NodePort and cluster IP extraction happens before any connectivity guard; if kubectl returns empty output (e.g., service not yet deployed), CLUSTER_IP and NODEPORT are empty strings and the script proceeds to write a broken MCP config URL ('http://:') before reaching the health check that would abort it.
proposed_fix: After lines 13-14, add explicit guards: '[[ -z "$CLUSTER_IP" ]] && { echo "ERROR: could not determine cluster IP"; exit 1; }' and '[[ -z "$NODEPORT" ]] && { echo "ERROR: could not determine NodePort"; exit 1; }'. Move the kubectl prereq check (kubectl cluster-info) to before the variable extraction block so failures are caught with clear messages.
test_plan: Run the script against a context with no matching service and verify it exits non-zero before writing any config file; verify that when CLUSTER_IP is empty the exit message is 'ERROR: could not determine cluster IP'.

evidence: src/mcp_server.py:484 — 'async def handle_call_tool(name: str, arguments: Optional[Dict[str, Any]])'; line 491 — 'arguments.get("include_details", True)' called without None-guard; line 608-611 — unknown tool name returns success-shaped CallToolResult with 'Unknown tool:' text instead of an MCP error; line 617-621 — bare except Exception swallows all errors and returns error text as a successful tool result (HTTP 200 equivalent), preventing MCP clients from detecting failures.
diagnosis: handle_call_tool has three critical gaps: (1) arguments can be None (optional) but is dereferenced without a None check causing AttributeError on tools with no arguments; (2) unknown tool names return a success response, not an MCP error; (3) all exceptions are caught and returned as success-shaped text, making failures invisible to MCP clients.
proposed_fix: Line 484: add 'arguments = arguments or {}' at start of handler. Lines 608-611: raise MCPError or return CallToolResult with isError=True for unknown tools. Lines 617-621: re-raise or return CallToolResult(isError=True, content=[TextContent(...)]) so MCP clients can detect failures. Also add input validation against each Tool's inputSchema before dispatch.
test_plan: Call handle_call_tool with name='get_cluster_info' and arguments=None; assert no AttributeError. Call with name='nonexistent_tool'; assert the returned CallToolResult has isError=True. Inject an exception in k8s_manager.get_cluster_info and assert the error result has isError=True.

evidence: src/github_issues_manager.py:488-490 — 'search_terms = " OR ".join([f"title LIKE \'%{term}%\' OR body LIKE \'%{term}%\'" for term in key_terms])' followed by 'sql = f"""SELECT ... WHERE ({search_terms})"""' — user-derived terms are interpolated directly into SQL with an f-string
diagnosis: find_similar_issues builds a SQL WHERE clause by string-interpolating key_terms extracted from the user-supplied error_message, creating a SQL injection vulnerability.
proposed_fix:
Replace f-string SQL construction with parameterized queries:
  placeholders = ' OR '.join(['title LIKE ? OR body LIKE ?' for _ in key_terms])
  sql = f'SELECT ... WHERE ({placeholders}) AND state = "closed" ORDER BY relevance_score DESC LIMIT ?'
  params = [val for term in key_terms for val in (f'%{term}%', f'%{term}%')] + [max_results]
  async with db.execute(sql, params) as cursor: ...
test_plan: Pass error_message="'; DROP TABLE github_issues; --" and assert the table still exists after the call; separately assert results return an empty list rather than raising an exception.

evidence: k8s/deployment.yaml:37 — 'image: ghcr.io/hawaiideveloper/mcp-kubernetes-platform-engineer:latest'; line 50 — 'value: "/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount"' set as KUBECONFIG; line 90 — 'readOnlyRootFilesystem: false'
diagnosis: Three security defects: (1) mutable :latest image tag blocks reproducibility and auditability; (2) KUBECONFIG is set to the service account directory path rather than the kubeconfig file path, which will cause client initialization to fail; (3) readOnlyRootFilesystem is false, allowing container filesystem writes.
proposed_fix:
1. Pin image: ghcr.io/hawaiideveloper/mcp-kubernetes-platform-engineer:1.0.0@sha256:<digest>
2. Remove the KUBECONFIG env var entirely — the Python kubernetes client auto-detects in-cluster config from the service account mount without KUBECONFIG.
3. Set readOnlyRootFilesystem: true and ensure data/logs are written to the already-defined volume mounts.
test_plan: Deploy to a test cluster; confirm pod starts without CrashLoopBackOff; confirm kubernetes client initializes by calling get_cluster_info and receiving real node data; run trivy image scan and assert no critical CVEs on pinned digest.

evidence: src/mcp_server.py:599-602 — handler calls self.github_issues_manager.get_trending_issues(); src/github_issues_manager.py:541-571 — method queries SQLite by created_at >= since_date; database is seeded by _fetch_recent_issues on startup which calls real GitHub API but has no auth token by default (line 56: 'self.github_token = None')

Read token from config or environment in __init__:

evidence: Design task — no existing implementation in src/; relevant future context in diagnostics_manager.py and security_manager.py which produce flat lists of findings with no deduplication logic
diagnosis: No finding deduplication exists; when implemented, the system will emit duplicate findings for the same root cause across multiple resources, creating alert fatigue.
proposed_fix: Data model: add a Finding dataclass with fields root_cause_key: str (hash of issue_type + message template), affected_resources: list[str], count: int, first_seen, last_seen. Interface: def dedup_findings(findings: list[Finding]) -> list[Finding] groups by root_cause_key and merges affected_resources lists, keeping the highest-severity instance. Dependencies: hashlib for key generation, existing diagnostics_manager output. Edge cases: same error message but distinct root causes (distinguish by resource_type prefix in key); findings from different namespaces that share root cause should merge with namespace listed per resource. Test plan: create 10 Finding objects with 3 distinct root_cause_keys; assert dedup_findings returns 3 findings each with correct affected_resources count.
test_plan: Unit test dedup_findings with synthetic finding lists containing intentional duplicates; assert output length equals unique root_cause_key count and affected_resources contains all original resource names.

evidence: Design task — no namespace guardrail exists anywhere in src/; src/k8s_manager.py:91+ execute_remediation accepts arbitrary target strings with no namespace filtering; ibkr-live-trader, daxxon-trading, brightflow-live namespaces receive no special treatment
diagnosis: No code prevents the auto-remediator from applying mutations (restart, patch, scale) to safety-critical trading namespaces without a PR gate, creating risk of unattended production financial system disruption.
proposed_fix: Data model: add BLOCKED_NAMESPACES = frozenset({'ibkr-live-trader','daxxon-trading','brightflow-live'}) in config.py. Interface: add a guard function check_namespace_block(namespace: str, dry_run: bool) -> None that raises NamespaceBlockedError if namespace in BLOCKED_NAMESPACES and not dry_run. Call this guard at the top of execute_remediation() and any kubectl_manager write operation before any cluster mutation. Edge cases: handle namespace=None (do not block), partial name matches (exact match only to avoid false positives), dry_run=True must still be allowed for inspection. Dependencies: config.py constant, custom exception class NamespaceBlockedError. The MCP tool response must surface the block reason to the caller.
test_plan: Unit test: call execute_remediation with namespace='ibkr-live-trader' and dry_run=False; assert NamespaceBlockedError is raised and no kubernetes API call is made. Confirm dry_run=True proceeds without error. Integration test: verify the error propagates as a structured MCP error response.

evidence: src/main.py:107-113 — GET /k8s/diagnostics returns hardcoded {"status": "healthy", "cluster_info": "Available", "nodes": "Ready", "pods": "Running"}; similarly /k8s/monitoring (line 122-126) and /k8s/security (line 133-138) return static strings. These endpoints never call self.mcp_server methods.
diagnosis: The three HTTP REST endpoints in main.py return hardcoded fake data and never delegate to any manager, making the HTTP API a hollow facade.
proposed_fix:
Replace each endpoint body with an actual call to the relevant manager:
  GET /k8s/diagnostics -> await app_instance.mcp_server.diagnostics_manager.diagnose_cluster_health(...)
  GET /k8s/monitoring  -> await app_instance.mcp_server.monitoring_manager.analyze_resource_usage(...)
  GET /k8s/security    -> await app_instance.mcp_server.security_manager.security_scan(...)
Also CORS allow_origins=["*"] at line 44 should be restricted to known origins.
test_plan: Start the HTTP server with a live kubeconfig; assert GET /k8s/diagnostics returns data whose node count matches `kubectl get nodes --no-headers | wc -l`. Assert non-"*" CORS origin rejection for unknown origins.

evidence: src/mcp_server.py:566-568 dispatches to documentation_manager.get_best_practices; src/documentation_manager.py:376-380 — method returns self.best_practices which is populated only from a hardcoded dict in _load_best_practices (lines 280-326), not from the scraped documentation_db.
diagnosis: get_best_practices returns a static hardcoded list that was never populated from real Kubernetes documentation; the live scrape at initialize() is completely bypassed for this path.

Or: store scraped best_practices keyed by category into self.best_practices during _build_documentation_index so the hardcoded fallback is supplemented by real data.
test_plan: Mock aiohttp to return a fixture page containing a 'best practice' sentence; after initialize(), call get_best_practices('security') and assert the fixture sentence appears in the returned list.

evidence: src/k8s_manager.py:25-34 initialize() contains comment '# Kubernetes client initialization would go here / # For now, we'll use a stub implementation' — self.client and self.api_client remain None. get_cluster_info (line 49) builds a fully fabricated dict with hardcoded values: "version": "v1.29.0", nodes total: 3, pods total: 45 (lines 50-74).
diagnosis: KubernetesManager never loads a kubeconfig or instantiates any kubernetes-client object; every public method returns hardcoded fictional data, meaning all cluster-facing MCP tools lie to the caller.
proposed_fix:
In initialize(), call kubernetes.config.load_incluster_config() with fallback to load_kube_config(). Assign self.core_v1 = kubernetes.client.CoreV1Api() and self.apps_v1 = kubernetes.client.AppsV1Api().
In get_cluster_info, replace the hardcoded dict with:
  nodes = self.core_v1.list_node()
  pods  = self.core_v1.list_pod_for_all_namespaces()
  ... aggregate real counts.
test_plan: Use unittest.mock to patch kubernetes.client.CoreV1Api; assert list_node() is called and its return value flows through to the response. Integration test against a kind cluster: node count in response must match `kubectl get nodes` output.

evidence: No existing implementation found in src/; design task for PRD.
diagnosis: There is no orphan-job detection or cleanup mechanism; the PRD needs a concrete design.
proposed_fix: Data model: OrphanJob { name, namespace, uid, creation_ts, owner_refs[] }. Detection: on a configurable schedule (default 10 min), call batch_v1.list_job_for_all_namespaces(), filter jobs where ownerReferences is empty AND labels do not contain a known controller label (e.g., app.kubernetes.io/managed-by). Interface: async def detect_orphan_jobs(namespace=None, age_threshold_minutes=60) -> List[OrphanJob]. Cleanup: async def cleanup_orphan_job(job: OrphanJob, dry_run=True) calls batch_v1.delete_namespaced_job with propagation_policy='Foreground'. Edge cases: CronJob-created jobs have ownerRef pointing to CronJob—must not be flagged; jobs in Terminating state skipped.
test_plan: Unit: mock list_job response with one job with no ownerRef older than threshold; assert it appears in detect result. Integration (kind): create a bare Job, run detector, assert it is found; run cleanup(dry_run=False), assert job is deleted.

evidence: k8s/namespace.yaml:1-9 — only a Namespace object with labels; no ResourceQuota, LimitRange, or PodSecurity admission label (e.g., pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted) is present.
diagnosis: The namespace manifest provides no resource governance or pod security enforcement, leaving the namespace open to unconstrained resource consumption and insecure pod specs.
proposed_fix:
Append to namespace.yaml or add companion files:
  1. Add label pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted to namespace metadata.
  2. Add a LimitRange with default cpu/memory requests and limits.
  3. Add a ResourceQuota capping total CPU, memory, and pod count for the namespace.
test_plan: Apply manifest to a test cluster; attempt to create a pod running as root—expect admission rejection. Attempt to create a pod exceeding the quota—expect quota error.

evidence: update.sh:1-5 — no 'set -euo pipefail'; line 3 calls ./stop.sh and line 5 calls ./start.sh with no error checking; line 4 silently swallows git pull failures via '2>/dev/null || echo ...'.
diagnosis: update.sh lacks shell safety flags and error propagation, so a failed stop, git pull, or start silently produces a partially-updated or dead server with exit code 0.
proposed_fix:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
echo 'Updating Kubernetes Platform Engineer MCP...'
./stop.sh
git pull origin main
./start.sh
echo 'MCP server updated and restarted'
test_plan: Make ./start.sh exit 1; run update.sh and assert it exits non-zero. Make git pull fail by removing remote; assert update.sh exits non-zero and does not call start.sh.

evidence: No existing vcluster integration found in src/ or k8s/; design task for PRD.
diagnosis: There is no vcluster sandbox lifecycle implementation; the PRD needs a concrete design for spin-up, fix application, smoke test, and teardown within ~10 seconds.
proposed_fix: Interface: async def run_in_vcluster_sandbox(fix_manifest: str, smoke_test_cmd: str, timeout_s=300) -> SandboxResult. Steps: (1) call 'vcluster create sandbox-<uuid> -n sandbox-ns --connect=false' via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec; (2) wait for vcluster pod Ready with a 60s timeout; (3) vcluster connect and apply fix_manifest via kubectl apply; (4) run smoke_test_cmd inside vcluster, capture exit code; (5) vcluster delete sandbox-<uuid> in a finally block. SandboxResult { success: bool, stdout: str, stderr: str, duration_s: float }. Edge cases: cleanup must run even on timeout (CancelledError); namespace must be pre-created with ResourceQuota to prevent runaway usage.
test_plan: Mock subprocess calls; assert create->connect->apply->test->delete sequence is always called in order and delete is called even when smoke test raises. Integration: run a no-op fix against a real kind cluster, assert SandboxResult.success=True and the vcluster is removed after.

evidence: src/mcp_server.py:539-544 dispatches to monitoring_manager.performance_analysis; src/monitoring_manager.py:148-190 — method comment says '# Stub implementation', timestamp hardcoded to '2025-08-09T10:30:00Z' (line 168), all _analyze_* helpers return fabricated static dicts (e.g., _analyze_cluster_performance lines 256-273 returns avg_response_time: '45ms', throughput: '1200 req/s' with no real data source).
diagnosis: performance_analysis returns entirely fabricated metrics with a hardcoded past timestamp; no metrics API, Prometheus, or kubectl top is ever called.
proposed_fix:
Replace stub helpers with real data sources:
  _analyze_cluster_performance: call metrics_v1beta1.list_node_metrics() (requires metrics-server) or query Prometheus API if configured.
  Use datetime.utcnow().isoformat() for timestamp.
  Return actual node CPU/memory from NodeMetrics objects.
  Raise NotImplementedError with a clear message if metrics-server is unavailable rather than returning fake data.
test_plan: Mock kubernetes.client.CustomObjectsApi to return a NodeMetrics fixture; assert cpu_avg in result derives from fixture values not hardcoded strings. Assert timestamp is within 5 seconds of test execution time.

evidence: No existing restart-ladder state machine found in src/; design task for PRD.
diagnosis: There is no restart-escalation ladder; the PRD needs a concrete state machine design.
proposed_fix: State machine with states: IDLE -> RESTARTING -> WAITING -> ESCALATED -> DONE. Transitions: enter RESTARTING by calling apps_v1.patch_namespaced_deployment with a rollout annotation; enter WAITING and poll deployment rollout status every 30s for up to 5 minutes; if rollout completes successfully -> DONE; if still degraded after 5 min -> ESCALATED (emit alert, optionally page oncall via configurable webhook). Interface: async def restart_ladder(deployment: str, namespace: str, escalation_hook: Optional[str] = None) -> LadderResult. LadderResult { final_state, elapsed_s, events: List[str] }. Edge cases: deployment not found (404 -> abort), no replicas available at any point (skip wait, escalate immediately), interrupted run (store state in a ConfigMap for recovery).
test_plan: Unit: mock patch and poll; simulate successful rollout within 3 min -> assert final_state=DONE. Simulate stalled rollout -> assert final_state=ESCALATED after 5 min timeout. Integration (kind): deploy a broken image, run ladder, assert escalation_hook is called.

evidence: src/mcp_server.py:571-574 dispatches to documentation_manager.find_commands; src/documentation_manager.py:382-399 — find_commands iterates self.documentation_db which is populated by _build_documentation_index via live HTTP scraping of kubernetes.io at startup. If scraping fails or is rate-limited (or network is unavailable in a cluster), documentation_db remains empty (line 36: self.documentation_db = {}), and find_commands silently returns [] with no error.
diagnosis: find_kubectl_commands silently returns an empty list whenever the kubernetes.io scrape fails at startup, providing no fallback and no indication to the caller that the data is absent.
proposed_fix: Add a bundled fallback: ship a static commands.json in the package with a curated set of common kubectl commands. In find_commands, if len(documentation_db) == 0, load and search the bundled file instead, and include a 'source': 'bundled_fallback' field in each result. Also log a warning when documentation_db is empty so operators know scraping failed.
test_plan: Block network access (mock aiohttp to raise ConnectionError); call initialize() then find_commands(command_type='kubectl', search_term='get pods'); assert result is non-empty and each item has source='bundled_fallback'.

evidence: src/diagnostics_manager.py:110-125 — results.update({"status": "CrashLoopBackOff", "restart_count": 5, "issues": [...], "suggestions": [...]}); lines 218-275 — _check_nodes/_check_pods/_check_services/_check_networking/_check_storage all return hardcoded static dicts with no k8s client call

Remove all hardcoded result dicts.

evidence: No HPAAnalyzer class exists anywhere in src/; diagnostics_manager.py and mcp_server.py contain no HPA analysis logic beyond the stub test at tests/production/test_network_service_issues.py:553-636 which mocks the resolution entirely.
diagnosis: HPAAnalyzer is completely unimplemented; there is no code that reads HPA objects, checks metrics-server availability, or detects the 'capped at max / never scaling' condition.
proposed_fix: Create src/hpa_analyzer.py: async def analyze(hpa_name, namespace) -> dict. Call autoscaling_v2.read_namespaced_horizontal_pod_autoscaler() to get current/desired replicas, scaling events, and metric sources. Call custom_metrics or metrics.k8s.io to read actual CPU/memory. Detect: (a) replicas == maxReplicas with CPU > threshold = 'capped'; (b) no ScaleUp events in last N minutes despite CPU > target = 'stalled'; (c) missing resource requests on target pods = 'metrics unavailable'. Return structured finding dict.
test_plan: Mock autoscaling_v2 returning a capped HPA and assert analyzer returns severity=high with diagnosis='capped_at_max'. Mock stalled HPA and assert 'stalled_no_scale_event'. Integration test against kind cluster with a real HPA.

evidence: src/logger.py:112-122 — get_logger returns logger.bind(name=name) where logger is the module-level loguru logger; type hint says -> logger (a module), not the actual return type (loguru.Logger). Line 93: logging.basicConfig(handlers=[InterceptHandler()], level=0, force=True) sets root logging level to 0 (NOTSET), meaning ALL stdlib log records including debug noise from urllib3/kubernetes SDK will flow through loguru. Lines 105-107: per-logger handler assignment but propagate=False means InterceptHandler at root is bypassed anyway.
diagnosis: Incorrect return type annotation, root log level of 0 enables extreme verbosity in production, and the InterceptHandler setup is logically inconsistent (root=0 but propagate=False on children defeats the intercept).
proposed_fix: Fix return type to -> 'loguru.Logger'. Change basicConfig level=0 to level=logging.WARNING or read from config. Remove per-logger handler overrides and instead set propagate=True on children so root InterceptHandler fires, or set each child's level explicitly. Add a LoguruLogger TypeAlias for the return annotation.
test_plan: Call get_logger('test') and assert the return type has .info/.error methods. Verify that after setup_logging(level='WARNING') a urllib3 DEBUG record does not appear in captured loguru output.

evidence: tests/production/test_network_service_issues.py:82-85 — k8s_manager.resolve_service_no_endpoints = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_resolution); resolution = await k8s_manager.resolve_service_no_endpoints(service_context). Pattern repeated for every test (lines 157-160, 232-235, 310-314, 383-387, 459-462, 540-543, 626-629, 699-702). File also references fixtures k8s_manager, network_manager, service_mesh_manager, storage_manager with no conftest.py defining them.

proposed_fix: Create tests/production/conftest.py defining k8s_manager, network_manager, service_mesh_manager, storage_manager fixtures that instantiate real manager objects with a mocked kubernetes client. Re-write each test to call the real method (not assign AsyncMock to it) and assert on the behavior of the actual implementation. Remove mock_resolution dicts and derive expected values from the mocked k8s API response.

evidence: No LLM backend abstraction exists in src/; no file references 'nim', 'nvidia', 'llm_backend', or 'cache' in the context of inference. The server produces text recommendations from hardcoded dicts only.
diagnosis: The pluggable LLM backend (NIM as default, cache by finding-hash) is entirely unimplemented; no interface, no NIM client, no caching layer exists.
proposed_fix: Create src/llm_backend.py: protocol class LLMBackend with async def complete(prompt: str, finding_hash: str) -> str. Implement NIMBackend(LLMBackend) using httpx to POST to NVIDIA NIM /v1/chat/completions. Add an in-process LRU cache keyed by sha256(finding_hash + prompt[:128]). Implement FallbackBackend that returns a static template string when NIM is unreachable. Wire into DiagnosticsManager so troubleshoot_pod_issues calls llm_backend.complete() with the finding dict as context.
test_plan: Unit test NIMBackend with a mocked httpx client asserting the correct endpoint and payload. Test cache hit by calling complete() twice with same finding_hash and asserting httpx called only once. Test FallbackBackend returns non-empty string when NIM raises ConnectionError.

evidence: No ServiceAnalyzer class exists in src/. diagnostics_manager._check_services() at line 244-253 returns a hardcoded dict with 'issues': [] — it never queries endpoints or checks selector-to-pod label alignment.
diagnosis: ServiceAnalyzer is unimplemented; no code inspects endpoint slices, compares service selector to pod labels, or detects selector mismatches.
proposed_fix: Create src/service_analyzer.py: async def analyze(service_name, namespace) -> dict. Call v1.read_namespaced_service() to get .spec.selector. Call v1.list_namespaced_endpoints() to get subsets. Call v1.list_namespaced_pod(label_selector=selector_string) to count matching pods. Detect: (a) endpoints.subsets is None/empty = 'no_endpoints'; (b) pod count > 0 but endpoints empty = 'selector_mismatch' (compare actual pod labels to service selector); (c) pods NotReady = 'pods_not_ready'. Return structured finding with diagnosis and remediation commands.

evidence: src/enhanced_tools.py:44-66 — kubectl_describe Tool schema defined. src/mcp_server.py lines 1-34 — no import of enhanced_tools. grep of mcp_server.py for 'enhanced_tools', 'kubectl_describe', 'get_enhanced' returns no output.

evidence: mcp-port-forward.sh:6 — set -euo pipefail (present, correct). Lines 56-57: kubectl port-forward ... & followed by sleep 3 (line 59) with no PID capture, no way to detect if kubectl exited immediately with error. Lines 100-101: curl called twice on health check — once for the -s /dev/null check and once to capture RESPONSE, doubling requests and creating a TOCTOU window. Lines 84-85: pgrep called twice to check then get PID, also a TOCTOU issue.
diagnosis: Port-forward background process is launched without PID tracking making orphan cleanup unreliable; duplicate curl calls and double-pgrep create minor TOCTOU races; sleep 3 is an arbitrary wait with no retry loop.
proposed_fix: Capture PID: kubectl port-forward ... & PF_PID=$!. Replace sleep 3 + test_connectivity with a poll loop: for i in $(seq 1 10); do sleep 1; curl -sf http://localhost:$LOCAL_PORT/health && break; done. Store PID to a file (echo $PF_PID > /tmp/mcp-pf.pid) so stop_port_forward can kill $(<"/tmp/mcp-pf.pid") reliably. Merge double curl in test_connectivity into a single call: RESPONSE=$(curl -sf ...) && echo "healthy: $RESPONSE".
test_plan: Run ./mcp-port-forward.sh start against a live cluster and confirm PID file is created and port is reachable within 10s. Run stop and verify the process is gone and PID file is removed. Run start when already running and verify the idempotency message appears without starting a second process.

evidence: src/enhanced_tools.py:162-191 — kubectl_scale Tool schema defined with resource/name/replicas required fields. src/mcp_server.py lines 1-34 — no import of enhanced_tools. grep of mcp_server.py for 'kubectl_scale', 'enhanced_tools', 'get_enhanced' returns no output.

Implement kubectl_manager.scale() using AppsV1Api.patch_namespaced_deployment_scale() or equivalent for replicaset/statefulset. Respect non_destructive_mode flag (raise PermissionError if enabled).

evidence: src/enhanced_tools.py:162-191 — kubectl_scale Tool defined; src/mcp_server.py:8-34 imports show no 'from enhanced_tools import' and grep for 'kubectl_scale' in mcp_server.py returns zero hits

evidence: src/security_manager.py:48-53 — 'overall_security_score': 85; lines 93-116 return hardcoded RBAC totals (total_roles: 15, total_role_bindings: 23); lines 119-150 return hardcoded pod counts (total_pods: 45); no kubernetes client import exists anywhere in the file
diagnosis: Every scan sub-method (_scan_rbac, _scan_pod_security, _scan_network_policies, _scan_secrets, _scan_container_images) returns entirely static hardcoded dictionaries with fabricated resource counts, CVEs, and compliance scores — no kubernetes API client is called.

Rewrite _scan_rbac to call rbac_api.list_cluster_role_binding_for_all_namespaces() and flag bindings with cluster-admin roleRef.
